mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the prior two quarters signals an easing monetary policy stance that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-08-01 vintage, near cycle peak
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 6-month change and forward 60-day SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive returns following declines are not statistically distinguishable from baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its 3-month prior low (a Sahm-rule style trigger), it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 3-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% as of 2024-08-01, up from cycle lows near 3.5%
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the signal's hit rate does not exceed the unconditional 65.5% positive-day base rate by a statistically significant margin.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth and a contraction in industrial production jointly indicate a broadening economic slowdown that is not captured by either series alone, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as recession probability repriced upward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 99.9757 below vintage 102.8887 indicating downward revision risk
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with rising recession risk
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce negative 60-day forward returns in less than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series individually in an ablation test.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime, declines in housing starts amplify equity market weakness because rate-sensitive residential investment contraction signals broader demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month specifically when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% AND HOUST declines > 5% over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% and HOUST revised value 1265.0 vs vintage 1238.0 as of mid-2024
failure_condition: Under the joint FEDFUNDS>5% and HOUST-decline trigger, forward 20-day SPX returns are not significantly more negative than the unconditional distribution (p>0.10), or the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS falls below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand that supports corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 698835 vs vintage 709668 as of 2024-07-01 showing continued nominal growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5-day SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production year-over-year growth exceeds 2%, it signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization consistent with a healthy supply-side expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings outlooks improve.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=102.8887 subject to medium revision risk
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show positive 20-day forward returns in less than 60% of cases, or no statistically significant difference from the unconditional 65.5% base rate.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a high federal funds rate and a low unemployment rate indicates an overheating economy at risk of further monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 5.33% while UNRATE 4.2% as of August 2024, near boundary of overheating regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: In joint-trigger periods, negative 60-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances, or removing the interaction term does not degrade predictive accuracy versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a deceleration in real GDP growth below its trailing 4-quarter average signals fading aggregate demand momentum that precedes equity market underperformance, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below trailing 4-quarter average
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised_value 23286.508 substantially above vintage_value 22924.863, reflecting high revision risk in this series
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth deceleration episodes fail to produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the effect reverses sign once controlling for FEDFUNDS trend.
